title: Classes & Objects 📦
description: Create your own custom data types.

slides.title: Creating a Class 🏗️
slides.content:
# Your First Class

Use the `class` keyword to define a class:

```
class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
```

**`__init__`** is the **constructor** — a special method that runs automatically when you create an object. It initializes the object's attributes.

**`self`** refers to the **current object** being created. It's how the object refers to itself.

```
my_dog = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
print(my_dog.name)   # Buddy
print(my_dog.breed)  # Golden Retriever
```

slides.title: Class Demo 🎬
slides.codeSnippet:
class Student:
    def __init__(self, name, age, grade):
        self.name = name
        self.age = age
        self.grade = grade

# Create objects
alice = Student("Alice", 20, "A")
bob = Student("Bob", 22, "B+")

print(f"{alice.name} is {alice.age}, grade: {alice.grade}")
print(f"{bob.name} is {bob.age}, grade: {bob.grade}")

# Modify attributes
alice.grade = "A+"
print(f"Updated: {alice.name}'s grade is {alice.grade}")

quizData.question: What is __init__?
quizData.explanation: __init__ is the constructor that runs automatically when creating a new object.

quizData.question: What does 'self' refer to?
quizData.explanation: 'self' refers to the specific object instance being created or acted upon.
